theme_and_archetype.description: Dark mode modern DevOps dashboard emphasizing tactical precision, high-contrast text, and 'command-center' functionality. Uses deep obsidian bases with highly visible electric blue and status colors.

typography.fonts_to_install: Chivo, IBM Plex Sans, JetBrains Mono

data_and_logs.usage: CRITICAL: Must be used for live logs, metric numbers, code snippets, Job IDs, and table data.

layout_and_spacing.grid_system: MODE B: THE CONTROL ROOM GRID
layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Tight padding inside widgets (`p-4` or `p-6`). Wide gaps between sections (`gap-4` or `gap-6`).
layout_and_spacing.radius: Use `rounded-none` or at most `rounded-sm` (2px) for a sharp, technical look. Do not use soft rounded corners.
layout_and_spacing.borders: Expose the skeleton. Use `border border-border` on all cards and panels. Add `border-l-2` with status colors (green/red) for status indicators on metric cards.

components_strategy.cards: Go cardless or use flat rectangular shapes with a 1px solid border (`border-border`). No soft shadows. Use `shadow-none`.
components_strategy.buttons: Flat, sharp edges. Primary actions use the Volt Blue (`#007AFF`) background. Secondary actions use `bg-secondary` with `border-border`.
components_strategy.tables: Dense rows. `text-sm` for data. Highlight the 'Status' column with appropriate status color text. Use Shadcn Table. No hover background on rows unless strictly necessary.
components_strategy.logs_panel: Terminal look. Background `#050505` or `#000000`. Monospace font (`JetBrains Mono`). Highlight INFO in blue, ERROR in red, WARN in yellow. Use a strict `border border-border` wrapper.
components_strategy.toast: Use Sonner. Dark background, sharp border, colored left edge based on success/error. Minimal animation.

motion_and_interaction.hover: Subtle background lightening (`hover:bg-zinc-800` or `hover:bg-secondary/80`). Text color MUST remain explicitly defined on hover.
motion_and_interaction.websocket_updates: When a row or metric updates via WS, flash the element's background briefly (e.g., `bg-zinc-800` transitioning to `bg-transparent`) to indicate data change.

icons_and_assets.icons_usage: Use stroke-width 1.5 or 2. Keep them consistent in size (`w-4 h-4` for inline, `w-5 h-5` for menu items).

images.description: Dark metallic abstract pattern. Use at 5-10% opacity for the main header background or as a subtle overlay on empty state containers.
Glowing circuit board texture. Use at 20% opacity and grayscale inside the empty state of the logs panel or when no jobs are selected.

accessibility_and_testing.testing_attributes: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include `data-testid` attribute. Convention: kebab-case, role-based. e.g., `data-testid='job-retry-button'`.
accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure text contrast against dark backgrounds meets APCA standards. Use text shadow over images if necessary.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: Set the dashboard entirely to Dark Mode. Do not implement a light mode toggle unless explicitly needed later. Overwrite the default `.dark` variables in index.css with the values provided in `css_vars_override`.
CRITICAL: Apply `JetBrains Mono` for ALL data tables, metric numbers, log outputs, and job identifiers.
Ensure the dashboard uses a dense, 'Control Room' grid layout with `border-collapse` style lines or 1px subtle borders between panels. No gaps or soft edges.
For the Live Logs Panel, implement a 'Retro-Futurism' terminal look: deep black background (`bg-black`), monospace font, and colored status logs.
Use standard `lucide-react` for system icons. Add standard `data-testid` tags to all filter inputs, action buttons, and table rows.
Keep border-radius strictly limited to `rounded-sm` (2px) or `rounded-none`. No soft, bubbly edges anywhere.

Use the abstract texture URL provided for the header background with a very low opacity (`opacity-[0.03]` or `opacity-[0.05]`) with `mix-blend-overlay` to give a subtle high-tech feel without distracting from the data.
